key_themes: Q2 2026 earnings & revenue beat (EPS $0.35 adj. vs. ~$0.27–0.31 est.; revenue $24.3M +8% YoY vs. ~$21.7M est.)
AI tailwinds boosting biosimulation/pharma modeling outlook and demand
Lowered FY2026 adj. EPS guidance ($0.75–0.85 vs. prior $1.03–1.10) due to higher tax rate; revenue guidance unchanged at $79–82M
Strong software revenue (60% of total, +9%), improved gross margins (~66%), and 18% services backlog growth
Stock trading at discount to ~$24 avg. analyst PT (Hold consensus) with notable upside potential
summary: Simulations Plus (NASDAQ: SLP), a provider of pharmaceutical modeling and simulation software, delivered a solid Q2 FY2026 beat with 8% revenue growth, strong adjusted EBITDA margins, and EPS exceeding estimates, prompting shares to inch higher or jump depending on the report.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/SLP/)[[2]](https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SLP)[[3]](https://www.zacks.com/stock/news/2897750/simulations-plus-slp-beats-q2-earnings-and-revenue-estimates) Management highlighted AI-driven opportunities, discovery/development strength, and backlog growth, though clinical revenue declined and full-year EPS guidance was trimmed purely for a higher expected tax rate (revenue and margin targets held steady).[[4]](https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/SLP)[[5]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/simulations-plus-inc-slp-q2-070026839.html) Overall investor and analyst sentiment is cautiously positive: the stock (~$14.37) sits well below average price targets of ~$24 (with some Buy ratings and “undervalued” models), but low projected growth and the guidance cut have kept the consensus at Hold.[[6]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NASDAQ/SLP/forecast/)[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/SLP/)
sources: 15+ web/news articles (Yahoo Finance, Zacks, Seeking Alpha, Motley Fool, GuruFocus, company releases) focused on April 2026 earnings, 5–10 relevant X posts on earnings/fundamentals (note: $SLP cashtag also heavily used for Axie Infinity crypto/gaming token, which showed separate bullish gaming narrative chatter), Analyst notes (TD Cowen PT cut to $16 Hold, MarketBeat/Zacks consensus, GuruFocus undervalued assessment)
